content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 22, 1990


Roy W. Lockwood
237 So. C Street
Oxnard, CA 93030

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-90-493

Dear Mr. Lockwood:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTIONS

1. Are you required to open a campaign bank account and file
the candidate intention and campaign bank account forms if your
only expenditure will be payment from personal funds for the
filing fee?

2. May the filing fee be paid with a cashier's check?

CONCLUSION

1. You are not required to open a campaign bank account and
file the candidate intention and campaign bank account statements
if your only expenditures will be from personal funds for the
filing fee or statement of qualifications.

2. You may pay the filing fee or the statement of
qualifications with either a personal check or with a cashier's
check.

FACTS

You are a candidate for the Oxnard Union High School
District's Board of Trustees. You do not intend to solicit or
receive any contributions. Your only expenditure will be for the
candidate's filing fee which you plan to pay out of personal
funds.

_______________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Roy W. Lockwood
Page Two

ANALYSIS

     Prior to the solicitation or receipt of any contribution or
loan, an individual who intends to be a candidate for an elective
office must file with the Commission a statement of intention to
be a candidate for a specific office. (Section 85200.) In
addition, candidates are required to open a campaign bank account
into which all contributions must be deposited and all
expenditures must be made. (Section 85201.) The term
"contribution" is defined to include the candidate's own money or
property used on behalf of his or her candidacy. (Section 82015.)

     However, Regulation 18522 (copy enclosed) provides that the
term "contribution" does not include payments from the candidate's
personal funds to pay a filing fee or a candidate statement fee.
Therefore, you are not required to file the candidate intention
statement (Form 501), open a campaign bank account, or file the
campaign bank account statement (Form 502) since your only
expenditure will be from personal funds for a filing fee.

     No monetary contribution or expenditure of $100 or more may
be made or received in cash. Payments of $100 or more must be
made by a check or other written instrument containing the names
of the payee and the donor. (Section 84300.) Therefore, you may
pay the filing fee with either a personal check or with a
cashier's check.


     If you have any questions regarding this letter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [illegible]

     By:  Alice Hughes
          Political Reform Consultant
          Technical Assistance and
          Analysis Division
Enclosure

sections.analysis:
Prior to the solicitation or receipt of any contribution or loan, an individual who intends to be a candidate for an elective office must file with the Commission a statement of intention to be a candidate for a specific office. (Section 85200.) In addition, candidates are required to open a campaign bank account into which all contributions must be deposited and all expenditures must be made. (Section 85201.) The term "contribution" is defined to include the candidate's own money or property used on behalf of his or her candidacy. (Section 82015.)

However, Regulation 18522 (copy enclosed) provides that the term "contribution" does not include payments from the candidate's personal funds to pay a filing fee or a candidate statement fee. Therefore, you are not required to file the candidate intention statement (Form 501), open a campaign bank account, or file the campaign bank account statement (Form 502) since your only expenditure will be from personal funds for a filing fee.

No monetary contribution or expenditure of $100 or more may be made or received in cash. Payments of $100 or more must be made by a check or other written instrument containing the names of the payee and the donor. (Section 84300.) Therefore, you may pay the filing fee with either a personal check or with a cashier's check.
